headline: Aldercrest Capital adds three to investment team after closing $400M raise from Midwestern pension
summary: Aldercrest Capital has hired a portfolio manager and two analysts following the completion of a $400M mandate from a Midwestern public pension. The new hires bring the firm's investment team to 12 professionals.

headline: Aldercrest's Q1 long/short book returns 8.2% net amid volatile equity markets
summary: Aldercrest Capital's flagship long/short equity fund returned 8.2% net in the first quarter of 2026, outperforming peers as the manager's industrial and technology shorts offset broad market volatility.

headline: Tiger-pedigree managers see renewed allocator interest as fundamentals trading regains favour
summary: Institutional allocators are showing renewed appetite for fundamental long/short equity managers with Tiger Management heritage, citing attractive valuations and the return of stock-picking alpha in the current macro environment.

headline: Pinehurst Pension Trust increases alternatives allocation target to 22% following board approval
summary: Pinehurst Pension Trust's board of trustees approved raising the alternatives target from 18% to 22% of total assets, representing approximately $4.2 billion in incremental deployment across private equity, credit, and real assets.

headline: Connecticut pension funds outperform national average in fiscal 2025 alternatives returns
summary: Public pension funds based in Connecticut delivered above-median alternatives returns in fiscal year 2025, benefiting from early allocations to direct lending and infrastructure that have appreciated as interest rates stabilised.

headline: Pinehurst names Diana Forsythe to lead expanded alternatives search process
summary: Pinehurst Pension Trust has appointed Diana Forsythe as Director of Investments with specific responsibility for the fund's expanded alternatives mandate. Forsythe joins from a Boston-based investment consultant.

headline: Vesper Macro Partners launches new emerging markets sleeve targeting EM rates and FX
summary: Vesper Macro Partners has officially launched a dedicated emerging markets strategy focused on EM interest rates and currency markets, targeting net returns of 12-15% with low correlation to developed market equity beta.

headline: Global macro funds post strong quarter as divergent central bank policies create trading opportunities
summary: Discretionary global macro funds returned an average of 5.1% in Q1 2026, capitalising on divergent monetary policy paths between the Federal Reserve, ECB, and Bank of Japan. Vesper Macro was among the outperformers.

headline: Vesper Macro's Ashford speaks at Managed Futures Summit on EM opportunity set
summary: Oliver Ashford outlined Vesper's thesis for emerging market rates at the Managed Futures Summit in London, arguing that differentiated inflation dynamics across EM offer multi-year alpha opportunity for discretionary macro managers.

headline: Mendoza Family Office hires former Goldman Sachs partner as Chief Investment Officer
summary: The Mendoza Family Office has appointed Claire Dupont, a former Goldman Sachs managing director and partner in the principal strategic investments group, as its new Chief Investment Officer. Dupont will oversee the office's $2.4 billion alternatives portfolio.

headline: Latin American family offices increasing allocations to global hedge funds and private credit
summary: Family offices with Latin American founding wealth are increasing their allocations to global hedge funds and private credit as they seek to diversify away from regional real estate concentration. Average alternatives allocation among surveyed offices rose to 31%.

headline: Albourne Partners expands family office coverage team amid growing demand for bespoke manager screening
summary: Albourne Partners has hired five additional consultants to its family office practice, responding to growing demand from single and multi-family offices for bespoke alternative manager screening and due diligence services.

headline: University endowments report 9.4% average return in fiscal 2025, led by venture capital
summary: The NACUBO-Commonfund Study of Endowments reported a 9.4% average return for university endowments in fiscal year 2025, with venture capital and private equity as the primary performance drivers for larger endowments above $1 billion.

headline: Larkspur Endowment's venture portfolio posts 22% return as three portfolio companies mark up significantly
summary: Larkspur's endowment venture capital portfolio delivered a 22% net return in fiscal 2025, driven by three material mark-ups in early-stage technology companies. The endowment is now accelerating its venture allocation as a result.

headline: Endowment model under scrutiny as liquidity demands rise at smaller institutions
summary: Several small and mid-size university endowments have reduced illiquid alternatives exposure after facing liquidity pressure during market dislocations. Larger endowments like Larkspur, with mature portfolios and diversified distribution requirements, are not seen as affected.

headline: Northwind Capital Partners wins $400M mandate from European sovereign wealth fund
summary: Northwind Capital Partners has been selected by a Northern European sovereign wealth fund to manage a $400M alternatives mandate covering private equity, hedge funds, and real assets. The win brings Northwind's total AUM to approximately $14.4 billion.

headline: Fund of funds managers adapting to new mandate as allocators seek co-investment access
summary: Traditional fund of funds managers are evolving their value propositions beyond diversification, offering co-investment deal flow and custom mandate structures as institutional allocators seek more direct exposure to underlying investments.

headline: Glenfield Insurance Group raises alternatives target as CEO signals strategic portfolio repositioning
summary: Glenfield Insurance Group's CEO signalled a strategic shift toward higher-yielding alternative investments in the company's annual report, confirming an increase in the alternatives allocation from 8% to 14% of general account assets over the next three years.

headline: Insurance general account managers flock to private credit amid tightening public bond spreads
summary: Insurance investment departments are accelerating allocations to private credit, particularly direct lending and CLO tranches, as public investment-grade bond spreads near historic tights. Regulatory capital treatment for investment-grade private credit remains favourable.

headline: Cobalt Bridge Foundation commits $250M to climate-focused alternatives portfolio
summary: Cobalt Bridge Foundation has made a $250M commitment to build a climate-focused alternatives portfolio, targeting venture capital funds with measurable emissions reduction strategies and infrastructure investments aligned with the foundation's net-zero mission.

headline: Impact investing inflows reach record $180B as foundations lead institutional adoption
summary: Institutional impact investment inflows reached a record $180 billion globally in 2025, with foundations and endowments accounting for over 40% of the growth. Demand for climate tech venture and impact-screened private credit led all categories.

headline: Brightwater Credit closes oversubscribed third direct-lending fund at $2.2B
summary: Brightwater Credit has held a final close on its third direct lending fund at $2.2 billion, exceeding the $1.8 billion target. The oversubscription reflects strong LP demand for middle-market private credit with a historically low default rate.

headline: Middle-market direct lenders outperform broader credit markets in 2025
summary: Middle-market direct lending funds delivered average net returns of 11.2% in 2025, outperforming broadly syndicated loans by 340 basis points. Conservative underwriting standards among top-tier managers like Brightwater Credit contributed to below-average loss rates.

headline: Brightwater Credit named to Institutional Investor's Private Credit 30 list for third consecutive year
summary: Brightwater Credit has been named to Institutional Investor's Private Credit 30 list for the third consecutive year, recognising the firm's consistent performance, LP service standards, and strong co-investment programme.

headline: Stonemoor Real Estate Partners closes Fund VII at $2.8B, exceeding target on Sun Belt demand
summary: Stonemoor Real Estate Partners has closed its seventh value-add fund at $2.8 billion, well above the $2.2 billion target. The fund will focus on industrial, multifamily, and mixed-use assets across Texas, Florida, and Arizona.

headline: Sun Belt real estate continues to outperform as migration tailwinds sustain rental demand
summary: Commercial real estate fundamentals across Sun Belt markets remain robust, with Dallas and Phoenix multifamily vacancy rates at five-year lows. Real estate managers with concentrated Sun Belt exposure have seen strong appreciation in recent vintages.

headline: Stonemoor's Karen Whitmore joins iConnections panel on capital formation trends in alternatives
summary: Karen Whitmore, Head of Capital Formation at Stonemoor Real Estate Partners, participated in an iConnections moderated panel discussion on evolving capital formation strategies in private real estate, highlighting the role of technology-enabled introductions in accelerating LP due diligence timelines.

headline: Halcyon Quantitative's systematic multi-strategy fund returns 14.7% in 2025
summary: Halcyon Quantitative's flagship systematic multi-strategy fund returned 14.7% net in 2025, ranking in the top decile of peers. The fund's machine-learning alpha signals across equities, rates, and commodities drove the outperformance.

headline: Halcyon expands alternative data team with six new hires from tech and finance
summary: Halcyon Quantitative has hired six alternative data specialists with backgrounds from Google, Microsoft, and two major investment banks. The expansion brings the firm's alternative data research team to 20 professionals, one of the largest in the hedge fund industry.

headline: Systematic funds attract record inflows as institutions seek uncorrelated alpha sources
summary: Systematic hedge funds attracted over $45 billion in net institutional inflows in Q1 2026, as pension funds and endowments sought diversified alpha sources amid rising equity valuations. Managers with machine-learning-driven strategies led inflows.

headline: Greenwich quant manager Halcyon exploring launch of institutional UCITS vehicle for European markets
summary: Halcyon Quantitative is reportedly in advanced discussions with a Dublin-based fund platform to launch a UCITS vehicle for its flagship systematic strategy, opening the fund to European institutional investors who face regulatory restrictions on offshore structures.

headline: Quartzlight Ventures targets $600M for Fund IV as early-stage B2B software market rebounds
summary: Quartzlight Ventures has formally launched fundraising for its fourth fund, targeting $600 million with a first close anticipated by Q3 2026. The fund will continue the firm's focus on early-stage B2B software, climate tech, and developer tools.

headline: Two Quartzlight portfolio companies cross $1B valuation threshold in separate funding rounds
summary: Two companies from Quartzlight's Fund II portfolio have crossed the unicorn valuation threshold following Series C and D funding rounds. The marks provide meaningful returns for Fund II LPs and strengthen Quartzlight's track record heading into the Fund IV raise.

headline: Climate tech VC attracts record LP interest as energy transition investment accelerates
summary: Venture capital funds with a climate technology focus attracted record limited partner commitments in early 2026, reflecting growing institutional conviction in the energy transition as both a financial opportunity and a mission-aligned investment.
